title: Telecommunications Engineering Specialists
description: Design or configure wired, wireless, and satellite communications systems for voice, video, and data services. Supervise installation, service, and maintenance.

tasks.text: Consult with users, administrators, and engineers to identify business and technical requirements for proposed system modifications or technology purchases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Implement system renovation projects in collaboration with technical staff, engineering consultants, installers, and vendors.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Keep abreast of changes in industry practices and emerging telecommunications technology by reviewing current literature, talking with colleagues, participating in educational programs, attending meetings or workshops, or participating in professional organizations or conferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review and evaluate requests from engineers, managers, and technicians for system modifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assess existing facilities' needs for new or modified telecommunications systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Develop, maintain, or implement telecommunications disaster recovery plans to ensure business continuity.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate with telecommunications vendors to obtain pricing and technical specifications for available hardware, software, or services.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect sites to determine physical configuration, such as device locations and conduit pathways.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Document procedures for hardware and software installation and use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, or coordinate installation of, new or modified hardware, software, or programming modules of telecommunications systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct in use of voice, video, and data communications systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Implement or perform preventive maintenance, backup, or recovery procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare purchase requisitions for computer hardware and software, networking and telecommunications equipment, test equipment, cabling, or tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Document technical specifications and operating standards for telecommunications equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide user support by diagnosing network and device problems and implementing technical or procedural solutions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Document user support activity, such as system problems, corrective actions, resolution status, and completed equipment installations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate costs for system or component implementation and operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order or maintain inventory of telecommunications equipment for customer premises equipment (CPE), facilities, access networks, or backbone networks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Work with personnel and facilities management staff to install, remove, or relocate user connectivity equipment and devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use computer-aided design (CAD) software to prepare or evaluate network diagrams, floor plans, or site configurations for existing facilities, renovations, or new systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare system activity and performance reports.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Implement controls to provide security for operating systems, software, and data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manage user access to systems and equipment through account management and password administration.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test and evaluate hardware and software to determine efficiency, reliability, or compatibility with existing systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Monitor and analyze system performance, such as network traffic, security, and capacity.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise maintenance of telecommunications equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
